title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV & IRL
desc: HBO's "The Pitt" is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack.

title: Cities Hosting Major Events Need More Focus on Wireless, Drone Defense
desc: Major events like the FIFA World Cup need to look beyond traditional physical and cyber security to active and passive wireless threats, say experts.

title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
desc: It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all.

title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
desc: Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers.

title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
desc: When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider.

title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
desc: The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind.

title: Chinese Police Use ChatGPT to Smear Japan PM Takaichi
desc: A Chinese keyboard warrior inadvertently leaked information about politically motivated influence operations through a ChatGPT account.

title: Flaws in Claude Code Put Developers' Machines at Risk
desc: The vulnerabilities highlight a big drawback to integrating AI into software development workflows and the potential impact on supply chains.

title: RAMP Forum Seizure Fractures Ransomware Ecosystem
desc: Researchers suggest defenders monitor how these malicious groups re-form and leverage the useful threat intel to guide their next moves.

title: PCI Council Says Threats to Payments Systems Are Speeding Up
desc: The PCI Security Standards Council experienced a record year in many regards, but its first annual report shows it needs to work even faster to stay ahead of attackers.

title: Malicious Next.js Repos Target Developers Via Fake Job Interviews
desc: Linked to North Korean fake job-recruitment campaigns, the poisoned repositories are aimed at establishing persistent access to infected machines.

title: Why 'Call This Number' TOAD Emails Beat Gateways
desc: Attackers are bypassing email gateways through telephone-oriented attack delivery (TOAD), in which the only email payload is a phone number.

title: 'Richter Scale' Model Measures Magnitude of OT Cyber Incidents
desc: ICS/OT experts have devised a scoring system for rating the severity and effects of cybersecurity events in operational technology environments.

title: Operation Red Card 2.0 Leads to 651 Arrests in Africa
desc: In the latest operation targeting cybercrime groups, African law enforcement agencies cooperated with Interpol and cybersecurity firms to recover more than $4.3 million.

title: Attackers Now Need Just 29 Minutes to Own a Network
desc: Credential misuse, AI tools, and security blind spots help attackers move through breached networks faster than ever, CrowdStrike finds.

title: Lazarus Group Picks a New Poison: Medusa Ransomware
desc: The North Korean threat group also leveraged Comebacker backdoor, Blindingcan RAT, and info stealer Infohook in its recent attacks.

title: As Cybersecurity Firms Chase AI, VC Market Skyrockets
desc: Investments in cybersecurity startups took off in 2025 as venture capital firms focused not just on AI-native tech but on talent as well.

title: Spitting Cash: ATM Jackpotting Attacks Surged in 2025
desc: The attacks cost banks more than $20 million in losses last year, as criminals used many of the same tools and tactics they have wielded for more than a decade.

title: More Than Dashboards: AI Decisions Must Be Provable
desc: AI systems have to be able to show a record of what happened and how.

title: Iran's MuddyWater Targets Orgs With Fresh Malware as Tensions Mount
desc: The long-active Iranian threat group debuted various attack strains and payloads in attacks against organizations in the Middle East and Africa.

title: Enigma Cipher Device Still Holds Secrets for Cyber Pros
desc: The Nazi relic's history is riddled with resilience errors, and those lessons still apply to defending against modern cyber threats.

title: 600+ FortiGate Devices Hacked by AI-Armed Amateur
desc: A Russian-speaking hacker used generative AI to compromise the FortiGate firewalls, targeting credentials and backups for possible follow-on ransomware attacks.

title: Attackers Use New Tool to Scan for React2Shell Exposure
desc: Researchers say threat actors wielded the sophisticated — and unfortunately named — toolkit to target high-value networks for React2Shell exploitation.

title: 'God-Like' Attack Machines: AI Agents Ignore Security Policies
desc: Microsoft Copilot recently summarized and leaked user emails; but any AI agent will go above and beyond to complete assigned tasks, even breaking through their carefully designed guardrails.

title: Lessons From AI Hacking: Every Model, Every Layer Is Risky
desc: After two years of finding flaws in AI infrastructure, two Wiz researchers advise security pros to worry less about prompt injection and more about vulnerabilities.

title: Latin America's Cyber Maturity Lags Threat Landscape
desc: The slower pace of upgrades has the unintended impact of creating a haven for attackers, especially for initial access brokers and ransomware gangs.

title: Emerging Chiplet Designs Spark Fresh Cybersecurity Challenges
desc: As scaled-down circuits with limited functions redefine computing for AI systems and autonomous vehicles, their flexibility demands new approaches to safeguard critical infrastructure.

title: Supply Chain Attack Secretly Installs OpenClaw for Cline Users
desc: The malicious version of Cline's npm package — 2.3.0 — was downloaded more than 4,000 times before it was removed.

title: Best-in-Class 'Starkiller' Phishing Kit Bypasses MFA
desc: A user-friendly PhaaS tool beats standard methods for detecting phishing attacks by live-proxying legitimate login sites.

title: Abu Dhabi Finance Week Exposed VIP Passport Details
desc: Unprotected cloud data sends the wrong signal at a time when the emirate's trying to attract investors and establish itself as a global financial center.

title: Connected & Compromised: When IoT Devices Turn Into Threats
desc: Reused passwords, a lack of network segmentation, and poor sanitization processes make the Internet of Things' attack surfaces more dangerous.

title: More Than 40% of South Africans Were Scammed in 2025
desc: Survey underscores the reality that scammers follow "scalable opportunities and low friction," rather than rich targets that tend to be better protected.

title: Scam Abuses Gemini Chatbots to Convince People to Buy Fake Crypto
desc: A convincing presale site for phony "Google Coin" features an AI assistant that engages victims with a slick sales pitch, funneling payment to attackers.

title: Critical Grandstream VoIP Bug Highlights SMB Security Blind Spot
desc: CVE-2026-2329 allows unauthenticated root-level access to SMB phone infrastructure, so attackers can intercept calls, commit toll fraud, and impersonate users.

title: Threat Intelligence Has a Human-Shaped Blind Spot
desc: How I realized what I was taught about threat intelligence was missing something crucial.

title: Dell's Hard-Coded Flaw: A Nation-State Goldmine
desc: A China-related attacker has exploited the vendor flaw since mid-2024, allowing it to move laterally, maintain persistent access, and deploy malware.

title: A CISO's Playbook for Defending Data Assets Against AI Scraping
desc: Discover a strategic approach to govern scraping risks, balance security with business growth, and safeguard intellectual capital from automated data harvesting.

title: Singapore & Its 4 Major Telcos Fend Off Chinese Hackers
desc: After detecting a zero-day attack, the country's effective response was attributed to the tight relationship between its government and private industry.

title: Supply Chain Attack Embeds Malware in Android Devices
desc: Keenadu downloads payloads that hijack browser searches, commit ad fraud, and execute other actions without user knowledge.

title: Poland Energy Survives Attack on Wind, Solar Infrastructure
desc: Russia-aligned groups are probable culprits behind the wiper attacks against renewable energy farms, a manufacturer, and a heating and power plant.
